outcome_contract.why: When guests message through Prime, staff have no pre-written answers to draw from. Every reply is written from scratch, even for common questions. The Prime app already has a rule-based Q&A agent with good answers and links, but that knowledge is not available to staff replying in the reception inbox. Making those answers available as templates means faster responses and consistently helpful answers.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Staff can select from template responses when replying to Prime messages, each giving a clear answer plus a link for more detail. When staff write a manual reply to a question no template covers, the system captures that as a candidate for a new template — building the knowledge base over time.

engineering_coverage.applicable: New TemplatePicker component in DraftReviewPanel, channel-gated to Prime threads
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same picker, plus a management UI for template CRUD
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: single new component, contained scope

engineering_coverage.applicable: Empty state (no match), keyword-matched suggestions, category browsing, one-click populate
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same, plus candidate promotion workflow states
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: three states (suggestions, browsing, empty), all client-side

engineering_coverage.applicable: N/A — internal staff tool, hardcoded text, no user input injection
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Requires auth on CRUD endpoints, input validation on template content
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: N/A — shared package is build-time code
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: N/A, no new attack surface

engineering_coverage.applicable: `templateUsed` field on draft tracks adoption; null value signals manual reply
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same, plus candidate capture logging in D1
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: existing `templateUsed` plumbing is sufficient for v1 observability

engineering_coverage.applicable: Pure function: message in, ranked templates out. Highly unit-testable. Integration test for picker-to-textarea flow.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Same unit tests plus API endpoint tests, migration tests, management UI tests
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Same unit tests plus cross-app integration tests for shared package
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: fewest test surfaces, highest confidence per test

engineering_coverage.applicable: New `PrimeTemplate` type. `InboxDraftUpdateInput` optionally extended with `templateUsed`. Static array, no schema migration.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: D1 schema migration for `prime_templates` table. New API contract for CRUD. Zod schemas for template input/output.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Shared package exports `PrimeTemplate` type. Both apps depend on package contract.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: one new type, optional field addition, no migration

engineering_coverage.applicable: Client-side O(n) keyword scan over <20 items — instant. No network calls for template data.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Server-side query on each thread open. Adds latency and a failure mode.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Deploy new code. Rollback = deploy previous version. No data to migrate or clean up.
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Deploy code + run D1 migration. Rollback requires migration rollback. Data cleanup if rolled back after templates are created.
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Deploy shared package + both consuming apps. Rollback requires coordinated deploy of both apps.
engineering_coverage.carry_forward_or_notes: Option A: simplest rollout — single app deploy, no migration, no coordination

open_operator_questions: Should templates include the full answerComposer answers as-is, or should staff-facing versions be rewritten?
Should templates be available for Prime broadcast threads too, or only Prime direct (support) threads?
Should the template picker auto-suggest based on the guest's latest message, or show a category-grouped list for manual browsing?

stage_payload.decision_summary: Staff replying to Prime guest messages write every response from scratch, despite the Prime app already containing a rule-based Q&A engine (`answerComposer.ts`) with well-written answers and links for the six most common question categories. This analysis determines how to surface those answers as selectable templates in the reception draft UI, with a path toward self-improvement over time. The decision is between three viable storage and delivery approaches: a static TypeScript module, a D1 database-backed system with a learning loop, and a shared package that reuses `answerComposer` directly
goals: Staff can select a template response when replying to common Prime guest questions, reducing reply time from minutes to seconds
Template selection is tracked (`templateUsed` field) so adoption can be measured
The architecture supports adding a self-improvement loop in a future phase without rework
non_goals: LLM-based template suggestion beyond keyword matching (Phase 2)
Self-improvement loop that captures manual replies as template candidates (Phase 2)
assumptions: Staff will adopt templates when they are prominent and one-click (supported by the pattern that auto-draft adoption is high for email threads)
The 6 answerComposer categories cover the most common Prime guest questions (supported by the 10 preset questions in Homepage.json covering the same topics)
Prime internal links (`/booking-details`, `/activities`, etc.) render as tappable links in Prime chat messages (supported by the `links` field in message objects)

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Staff are writing every reply from scratch today; faster delivery = faster ROI
Smaller scope = less risk, faster build, fewer moving parts
The long-term value is in growing the template set from manual replies
Templates change infrequently; avoid infrastructure overhead for a small dataset
Staff serve EN and IT guests; both languages needed
Approaches matching existing patterns are cheaper to review and maintain
Two sources of truth for the same answers create maintenance debt

options_considered.Description: Templates as a typed TS array in reception app, derived from answerComposer. Template picker in DraftReviewPanel. `templateUsed` tracked client-side.
options_considered.Upside: Simplest. No API, no DB, no migration. Build-time validated. Highly testable (pure function).
options_considered.Downside: Adding templates requires a code deploy. Two sources of truth with answerComposer.
options_considered.Key risks: Data drift between answerComposer and template file

options_considered.Option: B: D1 database-backed with learning loop
options_considered.Description: Templates in a D1 table with CRUD API. Keyword matching server-side. Manual replies flagged as candidates in same table. Staff promote candidates via management UI.
options_considered.Upside: Dynamic updates without deploy. Self-improvement loop is native. Single source of truth in DB.
options_considered.Downside: Requires D1 migration, new API endpoints, management UI, server-side matching logic. Significantly larger scope.
options_considered.Key risks: Over-engineering for <20 templates. Management UI is a separate feature.

options_considered.Description: Extract `answerComposer` logic into a shared package (`packages/prime-templates`). Both Prime app and reception import from it. Self-improvement feeds into shared package.
options_considered.Upside: Single source of truth. Both apps benefit from improvements.
options_considered.Downside: Couples Prime app and reception at the package level. `answerComposer` uses `i18n-exempt` strings — shared package would need i18n support added. Link format differs (Prime internal paths vs staff-facing context). Cross-app coupling for a small dataset.
options_considered.Key risks: Package coupling creates coordination overhead for changes. i18n retrofit of answerComposer is a separate workstream.

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — Static TypeScript module in the reception app
chosen_approach.Why this wins: It delivers the core value (staff can select pre-written answers for common questions) with the least complexity. The template dataset is small (<20 items), changes infrequently, and is derived from a stable, production-tested source (`answerComposer.ts`). The existing `templateUsed` plumbing in `InboxDraft` and `DraftReviewPanel` means the tracking infrastructure already exists. A static module is build-time validated, trivially testable (pure function: message text in, ranked suggestions out), and adds no new failure modes (no API calls, no DB queries, no migrations). The self-improvement lo
chosen_approach.Q: Should the template picker auto-suggest based on the guest's latest message, or show a category-grouped list for manual browsing?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: UX preference. Auto-suggest is smarter but may feel restrictive. Category browsing is simpler but requires more clicks.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: Both are feasible. The recommendation is to do both — auto-suggest first, with a "Browse all" fallback — but the operator may prefer simplicity.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (D1 database-backed)** — Over-engineered for the current dataset size and change frequency. Adds a D1 migration, CRUD API endpoints, and a management UI — each of which is a feature in its own right. The self-improvement loop it enables is explicitly out of scope for v1. If template volume grows significantly or dynamic updates become necessary, this can be introduced as a Phase 2 migration without rework (the `PrimeTemplate` type from Option A maps directly to a D1 table schema).

**Option C (Shared package)** — Creates cross-app coupling for a small dataset. The `answerComposer` in Prime is marked `i18n-exempt` and uses hardcoded EN strings; retrofitting it for i18n is a separate workstream. The link formats differ between guest-facing (Prime app) and staff-facing (reception) contexts. Shared packages in this codebase have proven coordination overhead (see TypeScript `paths` inheritance gotcha in memory). The benefit of a single source of truth does not justify the coupling cost when the dataset is 7 answers that change rarely.

sequencing_constraints: Template data file must be created before TemplatePicker component (data dependency)
IT translations can happen in parallel with component work
Draft save schema extension must happen before integration tests that verify `templateUsed` persistence
risks_to_carry_forward: IT translation quality — operator review needed before merge

The Prime draft save API (`PUT /api/review-thread-draft` on the Prime side) may not accept `templateUsed` — if not, tracking is client-side only (still visible in DraftReviewPanel badge, but not persisted server-side for Prime threads)

areas.label: Staff reply to Prime guest questions
areas.current: Staff reads question, mentally recalls answer, types full reply from scratch (2-5 min per reply)
areas.trigger: Staff opens a Prime thread in the reception inbox
areas.future: 1. Staff opens Prime thread. 2. TemplatePicker shows keyword-matched suggestions based on latest inbound message. 3. Staff clicks a template to populate the textarea (can edit before sending). 4. Staff clicks Save then Send. `templateUsed` is recorded. Total time: ~30 seconds for template matches.
areas.steady: Email thread draft flow is unchanged. Prime thread listing/sync is unchanged. Send flow is unchanged (only the content source changes).
areas.seams: If keyword matching gives poor suggestions for ambiguous messages, staff fall back to category browsing or manual typing.

areas.label: Template maintenance
areas.trigger: Operator identifies a new common question not covered by existing templates
areas.future: Operator (or agent) adds a new entry to the static TS template array and deploys.
areas.steady: answerComposer.ts in Prime app is unchanged — it remains the guest-facing Q&A engine. Templates are a separate, reception-specific data source.
areas.seams: Data drift between answerComposer and reception templates. Mitigated by periodic reconciliation. Future Phase 2 could unify via D1.

areas.label: Draft tracking
areas.current: `templateUsed` field exists on `InboxDraft` but is always null for Prime threads
areas.trigger: Staff selects a template before saving
areas.future: `templateUsed` is set to the template ID on save. DraftReviewPanel badge shows which template was used. Null value on sent drafts signals a manual reply (observable for future self-improvement).
areas.seams: `templateUsed` must flow through the save API — requires schema extension.
